## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Java JDK** | 17+ | Backend microservices runtime |
| **Maven Wrapper** | Bundled (`mvnw` / `mvnw.cmd`) | Build tool — no global Maven install required |
| **Node.js** | 18+ | Frontend build and dev server |
| **npm** | 9+ (bundled with Node.js) | Frontend package manager |
| **MongoDB** | 6.x+ | Database engine for all microservices |
| **Git** | 2.x+ | Source control |

### Optional

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Docker** | Running MongoDB via container instead of native install |
| **mongoimport** | Included with MongoDB tools — used for sample data import |

## 2. Service Inventory

| # | Service | Port | Type | Database | URL |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | service-registry | 8761 | Eureka Server | — | http://localhost:8761 |

> **Note**: `user-service` now reads from `purely_auth_service` (same as `auth-service`) — this is the BUG-1 fix.

---

## 3. MongoDB Setup

### Option A — Native Install (recommended for local dev)

1. Install MongoDB Community Edition 6.x+ from https://www.mongodb.com/try/download/community
2. Start the MongoDB server:

   **Windows (service)**:
   ```cmd
   net start MongoDB
   ```

   **Windows (manual)**:
   ```cmd
   mongod --dbpath C:\data\db
   ```

   MongoDB should be listening on `127.0.0.1:27017` (default, no auth).

3. **Import sample data** (from the workspace root):

   ```cmd
   mongoimport --db purely_category_service --collection categories --file sample-data\purely_category_service.categories.json --jsonArray
   mongoimport --db purely_product_service --collection products --file sample-data\purely_product_service.products.json --jsonArray
   ```

### Option B — Docker

```cmd
docker run -d --name purely-mongo -p 27017:27017 mongo:6
```

Then import sample data:

```cmd
docker cp sample-data\purely_category_service.categories.json purely-mongo:/tmp/categories.json
docker cp sample-data\purely_product_service.products.json purely-mongo:/tmp/products.json
docker exec purely-mongo mongoimport --db purely_category_service --collection categories --file /tmp/categories.json --jsonArray
docker exec purely-mongo mongoimport --db purely_product_service --collection products --file /tmp/products.json --jsonArray
```

---

## 4. Service Startup Order

Services must be started in the following order. Each service needs its own terminal window. All commands assume the workspace root directory.

### Step 1 — Service Registry (Eureka)

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\service-registry
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

Wait until you see `Started ServiceRegistryApplication` in the console. Verify at http://localhost:8761.

### Step 2 — API Gateway

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\api-gateway
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

Wait until registered with Eureka. Verify at http://localhost:8761 — `API-GATEWAY` should appear.

### Step 3 — Auth Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\auth-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 4 — User Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\user-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 5 — Product Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\product-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 6 — Category Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\category-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 7 — Cart Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\cart-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 8 — Order Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\order-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 9 — Notification Service

```cmd
cd microservice-backend\notification-service
mvnw.cmd spring-boot:run
```

### Step 10 — Frontend

```cmd
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

Frontend available at http://localhost:5173.

## 5. Health Check Verification

After all services are running:

| Check | Command / URL | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| Eureka Dashboard | http://localhost:8761 | All 8 services registered |
| API Gateway | `curl http://localhost:8080/actuator/health` | `{"status":"UP"}` |
| Category List | `curl http://localhost:8080/api/category-service/category/all` | JSON array of categories |
| Product List | `curl http://localhost:8080/api/product-service/product/all` | JSON array of products |
| Frontend | http://localhost:5173 | React app loads, products page renders |

---

## 6. Bug-Fix-Specific Verification

### BUG-1 — Cart API for Authenticated Users (US-001 / PURELY-16)

1. Register a new user via the frontend or directly:
   ```cmd
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/auth-service/auth/register -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"username\":\"testuser\",\"email\":\"test@example.com\",\"password\":\"Test@1234\"}"
   ```
2. Log in and obtain a JWT token.
3. Add an item to the cart:
   ```cmd
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/cart-service/cart/add -H "Authorization: Bearer <JWT_TOKEN>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"productId\":\"<PRODUCT_ID>\",\"quantity\":1}"
   ```
4. **Expected**: `201 Created` — no 404 error. The `user-service` now correctly finds the user in `purely_auth_service`.

### BUG-2 — NaN Rendering in Cart/Checkout (US-002–004 / PURELY-17–19)

1. Open the frontend at http://localhost:5173.
2. Add products to the cart.
3. Open the cart drawer — verify subtotal and item amounts display as formatted numbers (e.g., `$12.99`), not `NaN`.
4. Navigate to checkout — verify the same.
5. If the cart API fails or returns incomplete data, the cart should display `$0.00` instead of `NaN`.

### BUG-3 — Broken Product Images (US-005–007 / PURELY-20–22)

1. Open the products page at http://localhost:5173.
2. If any product has a missing or broken image URL, a grey placeholder image with a camera icon should appear instead of a broken image icon.
3. Add a product with a broken image to the cart — verify the placeholder renders in the cart drawer.
4. Proceed to checkout — verify the placeholder renders on the checkout page.

## 7. Frontend Testing

Run the test suite to validate all bug fixes:

```cmd
cd frontend
npm run test
```

**Expected**: 4 test files, 21 tests, all passing.

For coverage:

```cmd
npm run test:coverage
```

---

## 8. Shutdown

Stop services in reverse order (frontend first, then backend services, then Eureka last). In each terminal: press `Ctrl+C`.

If using Docker for MongoDB:

```cmd
docker stop purely-mongo
docker rm purely-mongo
```

---

## 9. Troubleshooting

| Issue | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `mvnw.cmd` permission denied | File not executable | Run `git update-index --chmod=+x mvnw` or use `mvn spring-boot:run` if Maven is installed globally |
| Service fails to register with Eureka | Eureka not started yet | Ensure service-registry is fully up before starting other services |
| `Connection refused` on port 27017 | MongoDB not running | Start MongoDB service or Docker container |
| Port already in use | Another process on the port | Find/kill the process: `netstat -ano \| findstr :<PORT>` then `taskkill /PID <PID> /F` |
| Cart returns 404 after login | User-service still pointed to old DB | Verify `user-service/src/main/resources/application.yml` has `purely_auth_service` |
| `npm install` audit warnings | Pre-existing upstream vulnerabilities | Not introduced by this change — safe to proceed |
| Frontend blank page | Backend services not running | Start all backend services before the frontend |
| CORS errors in browser | API gateway not running | Ensure api-gateway is started and registered with Eureka |
| First Maven build slow | Downloading dependencies | First run downloads all Maven dependencies — subsequent runs are cached |

---

## 10. Environment Variables

No custom environment variables are required for local development. All services use defaults configured in their `application.yml` / `application.properties` files:

| Variable | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `server.port` | Per-service (see inventory) | Configured in each service's application config |
| `spring.data.mongodb.uri` | `mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/<db_name>` | Per-service database |
| `eureka.client.serviceUrl.defaultZone` | `http://localhost:8761/eureka/` | All services register here |

The frontend Vite dev server uses the default proxy configuration in `vite.config.js` to route API requests to the gateway at port 8080.
